comments: Everyone answered the FNC correctly from last week, TV cannot recall seeing a wrong answer.
Tony Wilson was out of the studio in Maida Vale 4 mixing Budgie.

track_listing.track: Nothing Going On In The City

transcript.text: A new one. But this week, we start with this.
I don't think I saw one wrong answer this week.
I don't reckon I've seen one wrong answer at all.
This is the right one, and the lucky one as well.
It comes from Judith Gibbons of 27 Westway, Harpenden in Hertfordshire.
Congratulations, Judy. You get yourself a £10 record token.
Go to her writing. The answer is like this.
The answer to last week's Friday Night Connection was dogs.
The tracks you played were
Heart of Dogs by the Pink Floyd off Animals,
Sweet Thing by David Bowie from
Diamond Dogs
and Martha, My Dear from the Beatles' White Album
which was written by Paul McCartney
about his pet old English sheepdog called Martha.
And Judith of Harpenden in Hertfordshire
has put together her own Friday Night Connection as well
which of course is part of the deal.
Three bits of music
with a definite connection or theme contained therein.
That's it, the Friday Night Connection
put together by Judith Gibbons
at 27 Westway, Harpenden in Hertfordshire.
Could be tricky.
Could be easy.
Couldn't it, Judith?
21 minutes past 11.
If you think you know what the answer is,
well, it's all down to letting me know.
Any form of communication you care to employ,
send your answer to The Friday Rock Show,
The Friday Night Connection,
Radio 1, Broadcasting House,
London, W1A4WW.
Don't forget, though, put together your own Friday Night Connection as well.
Okay, three bits of music, a theme contained therein.
Lucky person gets a £10 record token.
A bit of a classic there from Ted Nugent, from the best of Ted Nugent, called Great Gonzo's, which is just out on the Epic label.
That was called Motor City Madhouse, and that was specially for a gentleman who's well into Nuge.
Leighton Rees, who lives in Merthyr Tydfil, and before that, something from another new album, also out on Epic Records.
It's by Molly Hatchett. The album is called Take No Prisoners. They never do.
The track was entitled Bloody Reunion, and that was specially for a lady who's 18 today, Louise Harley.
Lots of love from Dad and Gene and Martin and Simon
and all of the Prescott mob.
That's the track I'm playing in the memory of Mr Tommy Bolan
who died, what, five years ago, Friday the 4th of December,
which was a great shame.
He was a great guitarist, that guy.
He co-wrote that track with David Coverdale.
It's a track from an album that came out way back in 1975 by Deep Purple.
The album called Come Taste the Band.
The track was called I Need Love.
Loads of people wrote in asking for me to play a Bolan-type track.
And I'm really always happy to do that, because he was such a great guitarist.
But I'll just put all sort of one card out of the bag.
Phil Reynolds, Old Fallings Lane, in Wolverhampton, wants me to say hello to Martin, who's in Reading,
and Hugh and Andy and his family, and especially to Karen, even though it's all over, all my love.
And before that, you heard Bitches Sin and Down the Road.
Back to our archive session now, National Health, Agrippa.
That's Thin Lizzy from their album Renegade.
It's out on Vertigo, and it's called Angel of Death.
And before that, National Health.
From our session by them, recorded in 1976.
The track was entitled Agrippa.
And here's The Last by Bitches Sin.
Incredibly tight three-piece band out of the United States of America
who've got an album out on Arista.
They call the rods, so is the album.
The track was called Nothing Going On In The City.
And before that, The Last of Four from our repeat session by Bitches Sin,
who are Tony Tomkinson, Ian Toomey, Pete Toomey, Dave Newsham, and Tony Lease.
And they come from the Lake District area.
And incidentally, that track, Strangers on the Shore, is available,
not necessarily that version, on Heavy Metal Heroes,
a compilation album on heavy metal records.
DJ was my engineer tonight.
Thank you, DJ.
Tony Wilson was the producer.
He's not in the studio because he's mixing Budgie in Maida Vale 4.
I'll be ready for rock on tomorrow here on Radio 1.
Have yourself a good night.
I like the music tonight.
See you tomorrow.
Until then, from all of us on Radio 1,
this is Tommy Vance wishing you a good night.
God bless.
